title: 10 Herbal Creams For Hematuria
intro_desc: If you're experiencing hematuria, which is when you see blood in your urine, herbal creams can be a helpful remedy. These creams are made from plants that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the bleeding and discomfort. One example is Calendula officinalis, also known as marigold, which is known for its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin.  Using herbal creams for hematuria can be beneficial because they allow you to directly apply the healing properties of plants to the affected area. For instance, Urtica dioica, or stinging nettle, can be made into a cream that helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing.  Another herb, Echinacea purpurea, is often used to boost the immune system, but it can also be beneficial in the treatment of hematuria. Its anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce the bleeding and discomfort associated with this condition.  Herbal creams can be applied directly to the skin, which allows for faster absorption of the healing properties of the plants. This can be especially helpful if you're experiencing discomfort or pain from the bleeding.  Using herbal creams can also be a more natural alternative to some pharmaceutical treatments, which can have harsh side effects. By using plant-based remedies, you can help to reduce the risk of complications and promote a faster recovery.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contain triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and carotenoids as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce swelling and protect the bladder from damage. The triterpenoid saponins, particularly inulin and calenduloside, have been shown to have a soothing effect on the mucous membranes, which can help to calm bleeding in the urinary tract. The flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, have potent antioxidant properties that can help to reduce oxidative stress and promote healing in the bladder. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Calendula officinalis creams may help to alleviate symptoms of hematuria.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for hematuria
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Calendula officinalis flowers and 1 cup of carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Clean and dry the flowers.
Combine the Calendula flowers and carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Let it sit for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the jar daily.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the liquid mixture. Heat the mixture in a double boiler until the beeswax and shea butter melt.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool. Pour the mixture into small containers and let it harden. Your Calendula cream is now ready for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica creams contains active constituents like histamine, acetylcholine, and tannins, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help reduce inflammation and swelling in the urinary tract, which can cause hematuria. The tannins in Urtica dioica also have astringent properties, which help constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding. Histamine, on the other hand, can increase blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing the severity of hematuria. By applying Urtica dioica creams to the affected area, people may experience relief from hematuria symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal creams for hematuria
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried Urtica dioica leaves, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Make a tea by steeping the dried Urtica dioica leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Let it cool.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of simmering water.
Strain the Urtica dioica tea into the melted coconut oil mixture and add the vitamin E oil. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Store in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains alkylamides, caftaric acid, and chicoric acid as its key bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and irritation in the urinary tract. Chicoric acid also has antioxidant properties, which protect the body from damage caused by free radicals. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Echinacea purpurea creams may help reduce bleeding and inflammation in the urinary tract associated with hematuria. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Echinacea purpurea creams can help alleviate symptoms of hematuria.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for hematuria
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the beeswax, coconut oil, and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Echinacea purpurea flowers to the melted mixture and let it steep for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Add the vitamin E oil and mix well. Pour the mixture into a tin or a jar and let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense creams contains silicic acid, fumaric acid, and caffeic acid as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the urinary tract, making it easier to manage hematuria. Silicic acid, in particular, has been found to have astringent properties, which help stop bleeding and promote healing in the bladder and kidneys. The anti-inflammatory properties of caffeic acid and fumaric acid further contribute to reducing pain and discomfort associated with hematuria. By applying Equisetum arvense creams topically, individuals may experience relief from hematuria symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Equisetum arvense herbal creams for hematuria
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Equisetum arvense leaves and flowers, and 2 cups of water.
Combine the Equisetum arvense and water in a saucepan, and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes, then let cool.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, and discard the solids.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the cooled Equisetum arvense liquid with 1 cup of coconut oil or carrier oil in a small bowl, and stir well.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams contains active constituents like hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids, which work together to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Hypericin has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm irritated tissues in the urinary tract, while hyperforin has antioxidant properties that help to protect against cell damage. Flavonoids, on the other hand, have astringent properties that help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding. These properties make Hypericum perforatum creams effective in treating hematuria, a condition characterized by blood in the urine, by reducing the frequency and severity of bleeding episodes. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Hypericum perforatum creams can help to alleviate symptoms of hematuria and promote overall urinary health.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal creams for hematuria
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of dried Hypericum perforatum flowers and 1 cup of coconut oil in a clean glass bowl.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat until it reaches 120°F to 140°F.
Add the 2 cups of dried Hypericum perforatum flowers to the heated coconut oil and let it infuse for 2-3 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bowl, squeezing the cheesecloth to get all the liquid out.
Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of the Hypericum perforatum infused oil in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring until the beeswax is melted and the mixture is smooth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains bioactive constituents such as aloin, aloe-emodin, and anthraquinones. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and irritation in the urinary tract, a common cause of hematuria. The anthraquinones also have a natural antiseptic effect, which can help prevent infections in the urinary tract, further contributing to the reduction of hematuria. The gel of Aloe barbadensis has been shown to have a protective effect on the mucous membranes of the urinary tract, preventing further irritation and inflammation. By reducing inflammation and preventing infections, Aloe barbadensis creams can help alleviate hematuria and promote urinary tract health.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for hematuria
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup aloe vera gel, 1/4 cup coconut oil, 2 tablespoons beeswax, 2 tablespoons shea butter, 2 drops vitamin E oil, and 2 drops tea tree oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
In a separate bowl, mix aloe vera gel and shea butter until well combined.
Slowly add the melted oil mixture to the aloe vera mixture, stirring constantly until smooth.
Add vitamin E oil and tea tree oil, and mix well. Pour the mixture into a container and let cool before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago lanceolata creams contains aucubin and plantamajoside, which are bioactive constituents that have been found to have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to hematuria. Aucubin also has antioxidant properties, which can help protect the kidneys from damage caused by free radicals. Plantamajoside has been shown to have a protective effect on the kidneys by reducing oxidative stress and improving kidney function. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of these compounds in Plantago lanceolata creams may help alleviate symptoms of hematuria by reducing inflammation and protecting the kidneys.
remedies.image_alt: Plantago lanceolata herbal creams for hematuria
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Plantago lanceolata leaves. Rinse with water and chop them.
Combine the chopped Plantago lanceolata leaves with 2 cups of water in a saucepan. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl.
Let the mixture cool. Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a separate bowl.
Add the cooled Plantago lanceolata mixture to the beeswax and coconut oil mixture. Stir until the mixture thickens and forms a cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale creams contains a high concentration of allantoin, a potent anti-inflammatory agent that helps to reduce swelling and promote healing in the urinary tract. Allantoin also has a demulcent property, which forms a protective barrier on the bladder and urethra, soothing and calming irritated tissues. Additionally, the creams contain rosmarinic acid, a powerful antioxidant that helps to prevent damage to the mucous membranes and reduce inflammation. The anti-inflammatory and demulcent properties of Symphytum officinale creams make it an effective treatment for hematuria, a condition characterized by blood in the urine due to irritation or inflammation of the urinary tract. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Symphytum officinale creams can help to alleviate symptoms of hematuria and promote a healthy urinary tract.
remedies.image_alt: Symphytum officinale herbal creams for hematuria
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Symphytum officinale flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and a double boiler.
Combine 1 cup of Symphytum officinale flowers and 2 cups of water in a saucepan. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and let it steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture and set it aside to cool. Melt 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1 cup of coconut oil in the double boiler.
Add the cooled Symphytum officinale mixture, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and a few drops of essential oil (optional) to the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture.
Stir well and pour the mixture into a container. Let it cool and harden before using as a cream for hematuria relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains glycyrrhizin, a powerful bioactive compound that helps reduce inflammation in the urinary tract. Glycyrrhizin also has anti-oxidant properties that neutralize free radicals and prevent damage to the kidneys and bladder. The saponins present in Glycyrrhiza glabra creams help to reduce bleeding and inflammation in the urinary tract, making it an effective treatment for hematuria. The anti-inflammatory properties of licoricidin and licorisoflavan A in the cream also help to soothe and calm the bladder and urinary tract, reducing the risk of bleeding. By reducing inflammation and protecting the urinary tract, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can help to manage hematuria and promote overall urinary health.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for hematuria
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1 cup of glycerin, 1 cup of glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil, and 1 tablespoon of beeswax.
In a small saucepan, heat the distilled water over low heat.
In a separate bowl, mix the glycyrrhiza glabra root powder with the glycerin until well combined.
Once the water is warm, slowly add the glycyrrhiza glabra mixture to the saucepan and stir until combined.
Remove from heat and let cool. Add the vitamin E oil and beeswax, then stir until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture is smooth. Pour into a container and let cool completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium creams contains bioactive constituents like sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce swelling and repair damaged tissues in the urinary tract. The sesquiterpene lactones in Achillea millefolium creams, such as achillin and leucodin, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause infection and inflammation in the urinary tract. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in the cream have antioxidant properties that help protect the lining of the urinary tract from damage caused by oxidative stress. By reducing inflammation and protecting the urinary tract lining, Achillea millefolium creams may help alleviate hematuria, or blood in the urine, caused by urinary tract infections or inflammation.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal creams for hematuria
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of dried Achillea millefolium leaves, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of shea butter.
Combine dried Achillea millefolium leaves and coconut oil in a double boiler. Heat for 2-3 hours, or until the oil is infused.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a bowl. Discard the solids.
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler. Add the infused oil, vitamin E oil, and shea butter. Stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool. Once cooled, use a spatula to scoop out the desired amount and apply to affected area.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help with hematuria, which is blood in the urine. Some herbal teas, like chamomile and uva ursi, have been used to treat bladder issues. They may help reduce inflammation and prevent the blood from appearing in the urine. However, more research is needed to fully understand their effects.
faq_safe: While herbal teas can help manage hematuria symptoms, it's essential to consume them in moderation. Drinking them daily without proper guidance can lead to over-stimulation or adverse interactions with medications. It's also crucial to ensure the tea is free from contaminants and made from high-quality ingredients to minimize potential risks.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can take a few days to a week or two to show results in reducing hematuria. Some people may notice a decrease in blood in their urine within a few days, while others may see improvements after a week or two of consistent consumption. This can vary depending on the individual and the specific herbal tea being used.
faq_time: For hematuria, it's best to drink herbal tea in the morning. This helps the body start the day with a boost of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the urinary tract. Drinking it in the morning also gives your body time to absorb the benefits throughout the day.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for hematuria, a condition where blood appears in the urine. Certain herbs like goldenseal and juniper berries may increase blood pressure or worsen bleeding, which can be problematic for people taking medications like blood thinners or high blood pressure medications.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant and have blood in your urine, it's essential to be cautious with herbal teas. While some herbal teas, like peppermint and ginger, are generally considered safe, others like saw palmetto and uva ursi may worsen the condition. It's best to avoid them until you can get a proper diagnosis and treatment.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas, especially if you have hematuria, which is blood in the urine. Some herbal teas can pass into breast milk and affect your baby. If you still want to try herbal teas, look for ones that are known to be safe during breastfeeding.
title_2: 10 Natural Herbal Creams for Children Suffering from Hematuria
